q: During an action potential, what causes the rapid depolarization phase?
options: K+ rushes into the cell through voltage-gated potassium channels
Cl- is pumped out of the cell by the sodium-potassium pump
Voltage-gated Na+ channels open, allowing a rapid influx of Na+ into the axon
The myelin sheath temporarily becomes permeable to all ions
explanation: When the membrane potential reaches threshold (~-55 mV), voltage-gated Na+ channels open and Na+ floods in, rapidly depolarizing the membrane toward +40 mV.
